    implementations of all methods.

__doc__r	
Packetizerz
    Read binary packets from serial port. Packets are expected to be terminated
    with a TERMINATOR byte (null byte by default).
    The class also keeps track of the transport.
    
	bytearray
bufferr
__init__1
Packetizer.__init__c
Store transportN
Packetizer.connection_madec
Forget transportN)

	__class__r

z9Buffer received data, find TERMINATOR, call handle_packet

FramedPacketz
    Read binary packets. Packets are expected to have a start and stop marker.
    The class also keeps track of the transport.
    
	in_packetr

LineReaderzZ
    Read and write (Unicode) lines from/to serial port.
    The encoding is applied.
    s
utf-8
replacec
handle_line

ENCODING

LineReader.handle_packetc
z2Process one line - to be overridden by subclassingz-please implement functionality in handle_lineNr*
liner
LineReader.handle_linec
        Write text to the transport. ``text`` is a Unicode string and the encoding
        is applied before sending ans also the newline is append.
        N)
write
encoder;
textr
write_line
LineReader.write_lineN)
ReaderThreada
    Implement a serial port read loop and dispatch to a Protocol instance (like
    the asyncio.Protocol) but do it with threads.
    Calls to close() will close the serial port but it is also possible to just
    stop() this thread and continue the serial port instance otherwise.
    c
        Initialize thread.
        Note that the serial_instance' timeout is set to one second!
        Other settings are not changed.
        TN)
daemonr2
protocol_factory
alive
	threadingZ
Lock
_lockZ
Event
_connection_made
protocol)
serial_instancerC

ReaderThread.runc
Thread safe writing (uses lock)N)
ReaderThread.writec
z8Close the serial port and exit reader thread (uses lock)N)
closer
ReaderThread.closec
        Wait until connection is set up and return the transport and protocol
        instances.

zs        Enter context handler. May raise RuntimeError in case the connection
        could not be created.
        rU
